query:
Research the company "hormelfoods".

Research the company's recent financial performance and strategic situation: (1) Latest earnings results - specifically any revenue misses, P&L gaps, or margin pressures in the last 2 quarters, (2) Recent CEO changes or new C-suite appointments, (3) Updated or pulled guidance for upcoming fiscal year, (4) Recent cost-cutting initiatives, restructuring, or strategic reviews announced, (5) Production/manufacturing growth rates or capacity challenges, (6) New product launches, platform expansions, or market entries, (7) Company size/revenue scale, (8) Industry vertical and go-to-market complexity (multi-channel, pricing-dependent, production-constrained), (9) Cross-functional operational challenges (media/marketing effectiveness, pricing power, distribution expansion, production capacity interactions).

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
Hormel Foods is navigating a highly transitional period in fiscal 2026, characterized by a massive executive leadership overhaul, significant corporate restructuring, and persistent macroeconomic headwinds. Operating at a $12.1 billion revenue scale, the company delivered mixed financial results in the first half of 2026—missing Q1 revenue estimates but beating Q2 expectations—while battling severe commodity inflation and logistics disruptions. To protect margins, Hormel has executed multiple waves of pricing actions, divested its whole-bird turkey business, and initiated a corporate restructuring plan that includes eliminating 250 positions and overhauling its manufacturing footprint. 

**Financial Performance, Scale, and Guidance**
Hormel Foods operates at a massive scale, reporting $12.106 billion in annual revenue for fiscal 2025 (a 1.55% year-over-year increase) and $12.145 billion for the trailing twelve months ending January 31, 2026 [Hormel Foods Revenue 2012-2026 | HRL | MacroTrends]. 

Recent earnings highlight a volatile operating environment marked by margin pressures and mixed top-line results:
*   **Q4 2025:** The company reported a GAAP loss, impairments, and margin compression, though it issued an upbeat outlook for 2026 [Hormel Foods (HRL) Stock Rises After Q4 2025 Earnings Miss but Strong 2026 Guidance].
*   **Q1 2026:** Net sales reached $3.03 billion (2% organic growth), missing analyst estimates of $3.07 billion [Hormel Foods earnings on deck as margin recovery test looms By Investing.com]. However, adjusted diluted EPS of $0.34 beat the $0.32 consensus [Hormel Foods earnings on deck as margin recovery test looms By Investing.com]. The Retail segment suffered a 19% profit decline driven by lower sales, higher raw material costs, and increased logistics expenses [Hormel Earnings Release Q1 2026].
*   **Q2 2026:** Net sales of $2.97 billion slightly beat the $2.96 billion expectation [Earnings call transcript: Hormel Foods Q2 2026 earnings beat forecasts, stock surges By Investing.com]. Adjusted EPS of $0.40 beat estimates of $0.35–$0.36 [Hormel Foods (HRL) Earnings: Latest Report, Earnings Call & Financials]. Despite the top-line beat, net income fell 12% year-over-year to $158 million, and Retail unit sales were virtually flat ($1.79 billion) with volume down 2.1% to 663 million pounds [Hormel Foods Q2 2026: Retail Sales Flat, Foodservice Up, Earnings Down 12%].

**Updated Guidance:** Hormel lowered its full-year 2026 GAAP diluted EPS guidance to $1.28–$1.37 (down from $1.37–$1.46) to absorb a $61 million loss related to a recent divestiture [Hormel Foods (HRL) Earnings Report Q2 2026 | Miss, EPS & Revenue | 24/7 Wall St.]. However, the company reaffirmed its FY26 net sales guidance of $12.2 billion to $12.5 billion and its adjusted diluted EPS guidance of $1.43 to $1.51 [Hormel Earnings Release Q1 2026].

**Leadership and C-Suite Overhaul**
Hormel is undergoing a near-total transformation of its executive suite. The board plans to install a permanent CEO in October 2026 [Hormel Foods brings back former CEO to serve on an interim basis | Food Dive]. Recent appointments and departures include:
*   **CEO & President:** Jim Snee retired as Chairman, President, and CEO on July 14, 2025, after 36 years. Former CEO Jeffrey M. Ettinger returned as interim CEO for a 15-month period, while John Ghingo was promoted to President [Hormel Foods Names New President; Interim CEO | Progressive Grocer] [Hormel Foods promotes John Ghingo to president | The National Provisioner] [Hormel Foods - Hormel Foods Announces Elevation of John Ghingo to President...].
*   **Finance:** CFO Jacinth Smiley departed in October 2025, replaced by Paul Kuehneman as interim CFO and controller [Hormel Foods Corporation Announces Leadership Appointment and Provides Business Updates | Morningstar] [Hormel Foods Corporation Announces Leadership Appointment and Provides Business Updates - Hormel Foods].
*   **Operations & Tech:** Donald Monk was appointed as the company's first-ever Chief Technology Officer (effective March 23, 2026), and Will Bonifant was named Chief Supply Chain Officer on February 12, 2026 [Hormel Foods hires first chief technology officer | HRL Stock News].
*   **Retail & Marketing:** Domenic Borrelli became EVP of Retail on February 23, 2026 [Hormel Foods Announces Upcoming Retirements of Mark Morey and Paul Peil - Hormel Foods]. Jason Levine was appointed CMO in December 2025 [Hormel Foods Corp Executive & Employee Information - GlobalData]. Lisa Selk was named SVP of Brand Fuel, replacing Scott Aakre who transitioned to Retail CMO [Hormel Foods Appoints Lisa Selk Senior Vice President of Brand Fuel - Hormel Foods].
*   **Board:** William A. Newlands was elected Chairman of the Board in February 2025 [Hormel Foods Corp Executive & Employee Information - GlobalData].

**Restructuring and Strategic Reviews**
To align resources and combat margin compression, Hormel announced a corporate restructuring plan on November 4, 2025 [Hormel Foods Announces Corporate Restructuring to Support Strategic Priorities and Long-Term Growth - Hormel Foods]. 
*   **Headcount Reductions:** The company is eliminating approximately 250 corporate, sales, and procurement positions through layoffs, early retirements, and closing open roles [Hormel Foods (NYSE: HRL) to incur $20-$25M restructuring charges under corporate reorg] [Why Hormel’s Layoffs Will Cost You $45,000 Per Month]. 
*   **Financial Impact:** Restructuring charges of $20 million to $25 million were expected across Q4 2025 and Q1 2026, with an additional $9 million to $12 million expected later in FY26 [Hormel cutting 250 positions with corporate restructuring | MEAT+POULTRY] [Hormel Foods forecasts annual profit above estimates on higher prices | Reuters].
*   **Divestitures:** On April 24, 2026, Hormel completed the sale of its whole-bird turkey business (including a plant and related assets) to Life-Science Innovations, which is expected to reduce FY26 net sales by ~$50 million. Hormel retained the "tom" portion and the Jennie-O brand [Hormel Foods earnings on deck as margin recovery test looms By Investing.com] [Hormel Earnings Release Q1 2026] [Hormel Foods at CAGNY 2026: Strategic Growth Amid Challenges].
*   **Compensation Overhaul:** Hormel is amending its pension formula to reduce pay credits after December 31, 2025, discontinuing contributions to the Joint Earnings Profit Sharing Trust, and terminating its Operators’ Share Plan in favor of base salary increases and a new profit-sharing program [Hormel Foods (NYSE: HRL) highlights 2025 results, pay and 2026 plan and strategy].

**Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Operational Challenges**
Hormel faces a highly complex, production-constrained operating environment characterized by severe commodity inflation and logistics hurdles.
*   **Capacity & Footprint Optimization:** Under its multiyear "Transform and Modernize" (T&M) initiative—expected to deliver $100M–$150M in benefits by the end of FY25—Hormel is investing $200 million to modernize its supply chain [Hormel Foods further optimizes manufacturing footprint | Supply Chain Dive] [Why Hormel is investing $200M in its supply chain | Supply Chain Dive]. This includes converting a Barron, Wisconsin plant into a value-added facility and a $30 million expansion at the Jennie-O Willmar, Minnesota facility (completing late 2025/early 2026) [Jennie-O Breaks Ground on $30 Million Willmar Plant Expansion]. Conversely, Hormel discontinued bacon production at its Tucker, Georgia plant (laying off 135 workers) due to aging equipment and partially closed another facility to reallocate volume [Hormel Foods further optimizes manufacturing footprint | Supply Chain Dive] [Hormel Foods Corp. Layoffs - TheLayoff.com] [Hormel Foods Reports Third Quarter Fiscal 2025 Results].
*   **Cross-Functional Challenges (Pricing vs. Costs):** The company is battling persistent headwinds from high beef and elevated nut costs, expected to last throughout FY26 [HORMEL FOODS CORP Earnings Call Transcript FY26 Q1]. Additionally, severe winter weather and industry-wide carrier exits caused an unexpected spike in logistics expenses in late Q1 2026 [Hormel Foods Corporation Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary]. To protect margins, Hormel implemented strategic pricing actions in early FY26 and a second wave of retail pricing at the start of Q2 2026 [Hormel Foods faces transportation cost pressures | Supply Chain Dive] [Hormel Foods Corporation Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary].
*   **Technology Integration:** To improve cross-functional execution, Hormel fully integrated the o9 Digital Brain AI platform across more than 70 global sites to automate demand and supply planning [What Hormel Foods (HRL)'s AI Supply Chain Upgrade and Index Exit Means For Shareholders].

**Gaps and Limitations**
Based on the provided research findings, the following requested dimensions could not be addressed:
1.  **New Product Launches & Market Entries:** The findings note capacity expansions for existing platforms (Hormel Fire Braised, Applegate) but do not detail any specific new product launches, platform expansions, or new geographic market entries over the last 6 months.
2.  **Media/Marketing Effectiveness:** While the appointment of a new CMO is noted, there is no data provided regarding marketing ROI, media effectiveness, or specific go-to-market campaign performance.
